findings.summary: MiniMax row split reaches block 0 then loses the Level Zero device on Q8_0 attention matvec before expert dispatch.
llama-bench uses semicolon to combine -ot overrides in one benchmark; comma creates separate benchmark variants.
Layer split avoids split-buffer Q8 but fails during model loading because ggml requests large per-device model buffers.
A single visible B70 can allocate 24 GiB, while four visible B70s fail a simultaneous 8 GiB per-GPU allocation at GPU 1 in the probe.

localmaxxing.reason: No real MiniMax generation benchmark has completed.

nextSteps: Prioritize MiniMax row split because it uses smaller per-tensor allocations.
Implement a focused Q8 attention fallback or temporary simple-buffer path for MiniMax row split.
Use tools/sycl_alloc_probe.cpp to reproduce and narrow the multi-device large allocation behavior before patching ggml allocator behavior.
